title: Maxillaria iridifolia
taxonomy: Maxillaria iridifolia is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Maxillarioideae. Maxillaria iridifolia is further classified as the genus Maxillaria and the tribe Maxillarieae. The exact classification of this species is within the subfamily Maxillarioideae of the family Orchidaceae. Maxillaria iridifolia is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Maxillaria iridifolia is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, specifically known for its unique, iridescence-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its reported analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria iridifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Maxillaria iridifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
